evidence_quotes.quote: I always felt it was unfair for the consumers to buy a product and later down the line being unable to access that product because of said product being limited to accessing the publisher's servers only in order to play, when it really did not have to be.

We could make those games playable peer to peer like in the early days of Online gaming or allowing players to create their own servers, but somehow the publishers decided to remove that possibility and force players to go trough the publishers own servers Only.

paragraphs.text: I am an Ex Ubisoft Employee who worked on Tom Clancy's : The Division. I've always dreaded the day that game would turn 10 years old because the would mean Ubisoft could turn off the plugs at any moment and the game I worked on and millions of players bought would essentialy become simply unaccessible and unplayable at all, basicaly meaning the death of the game itself. I always felt it was unfair for the consumers to buy a product and later down the line being unable to access that product because of said product being limited to accessing the publisher's servers only in order to play, when it really did not have to be. We could make those games playable peer to peer like in the early days of Online gaming or allowing players to create their own servers, but somehow the publishers decided to remove that possibility and force players to go trough the publishers own servers Only. This should not be the case and players should be able to continue playing those games by creating their own servers like Online gaming allowed in the late 90's and early 00's.
